title: Conference Scope
text:
The Conference aims to promote scientific exchange and interdisciplinary collaboration in the field of bioactive molecules and their innovative biotechnological applications.
We welcome original research contributions related to, but not limited to, the following scientific themes.

title: Submission
section: 1st Hybrid National Seminar on Bioactive Molecules
text:
1st Hybrid National Seminar on Bioactive Molecules

From Characterization to Innovative Biotechnological Applications

Digital biotechnology

The First National Conference on Bioactive Molecules: From Characterization to Innovative Biotechnological Applications (NCBM26) is organized by the Conception and Application of Bioactive Molecules (CAMBio) Laboratory in partnership with the Higher National School of Biotechnology (ENSB).
Held in hybrid format, NCBM26 will bring together researchers, academics, doctoral students, and industry professionals to showcase the latest advances in bioactive molecules and their biotechnological applications through keynote lectures, oral and poster presentations, and high-level scientific discussions.

Welcome
The Concept and Application of Bioactive Molecules (CAMBio) Laboratory, in partnership with the National Higher School of Biotechnology (ENSB), is pleased to welcome you to the 1st Hybrid National Seminar on Bioactive Molecules: From Characterization to Innovative Biotechnological Applications.
This inaugural seminar will bring together researchers, academics, students, and industrial partners to exchange recent advances in bioactive molecules and their innovative applications in biotechnology.
The seminar will be held in hybrid format, allowing participants to attend either on-site at ENSB or online.

title: Venue
text:
The The First National Conference on Bioactive Molecules (NCBM 2026) will be held in hybrid format, allowing participants to attend in person at the Higher National School of Biotechnology (ENSB), Constantine, Algeria, or online from anywhere in the world.



Conference Venue






TipHigher National School of Biotechnology (ENSB)



École Nationale Supérieure de Biotechnologie (ENSB) – Taoufik Khaznadar
📍 Ville Universitaire Ali Mendjeli
BP E66 RP 25.100
Constantine, Algeria
☎ Telephone: +213 (0)31 786 011
📠 Fax: +213 (0)31 786 010
🌐 Website: https://www.ensbiotech.edu.dz





About ENSB
The Higher National School of Biotechnology (ENSB) is one of Algeria’s leading institutions dedicated to biotechnology education, scientific research, and innovation.
Located within the Ali Mendjeli University Campus, ENSB offers modern teaching facilities, advanced laboratories, and an inspiring academic environment. Its location in Constantine, one of Algeria’s major scientific and cultural centers, makes it an ideal venue for bringing together researchers, academics, students, and industry professionals.



Participation






Important🏛️ On-site Participation



Participants attending in person will be able to:

Attend keynote lectures
Present oral and poster communications
Participate in scientific discussions
Meet researchers and industry professionals
Build new collaborations and networks









Note💻 Online Participation



Remote participants will receive:

Secure access to the online platform
Live streaming of scientific sessions
Electronic conference materials
Certificate of participation






Getting to Constantine
Constantine is easily accessible from all regions of Algeria.

✈️ Mohamed Boudiaf International Airport
🚆 National railway services
🚌 Intercity bus network
🚖 Taxi services

ENSB is located in the Ali Mendjeli University Campus, approximately 20 minutes from Constantine city centre and the international airport.



Accommodation
Participants are encouraged to book their accommodation as early as possible, especially during the conference period.
Several hotels and university residences are available in Constantine, offering a range of options to suit different budgets. The following accommodations are conveniently located near the conference venue or provide easy access to the Higher National School of Biotechnology (ENSB).

Interactive Map






Note



Locate the conference venue on Google Maps:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/QqjKPDUBc1EEHGoT8





Discover Constantine

Known as the City of Bridges, Constantine is one of the oldest and most fascinating cities in North Africa.
During your stay, you may wish to visit:

🌉 Sidi M’Cid Suspension Bridge
🏛️ Palace of Ahmed Bey
🕌 Emir Abdelkader Mosque
🏺 Cirta National Museum
🌄 Rhumel Gorges
🍽️ Traditional restaurants and cafés




Contact
For any questions regarding travel, accommodation, or the venue, please contact:
Conference Secretariat
📧 ncbm@ensbiotech.edu.dz
☎ +213 5 50 84 74 87







ImportantWe Look Forward to Welcoming You!



Whether you join us in Constantine or online, we look forward to welcoming you to the The First National Conference on Bioactive Molecules (NCBM 2026) and wish you a productive and enjoyable scientific experience.

title: Scientific Program
text:
Scientific Themes
The scientific program is organized around the following thematic areas:

Pharmacology of Bioactive Molecules and Citizen Health
Bioactive Molecules for Food Security and Agro-industry
Microbial Biotechnologies and Energy Security
Environmental Valorization and Preservation
Digital Innovation and Artificial Intelligence in Biotechnology








NoteProgram Updates



The detailed scientific program, including keynote speakers, oral presentations, and poster sessions, will be published after the abstract acceptance notifications.

title: Registration
text:
We are pleased to invite researchers, academics, students, and professionals to participate in the first National Conference on Bioactive Molecules: From Characterization to Innovative Biotechnological Applications.
The sonference will be held in hybrid format, allowing participants to attend either in person at the Higher National School of Biotechnology (ENSB), Constantine, or online.

title: Registration
text:
Frequently Asked Questions

Can I participate online?
Yes. The conference is organized in a hybrid format, allowing both in-person and online participation.


Is a certificate provided?
Yes. Every registered participant will receive an official Certificate of Participation.


Can I attend without presenting a paper?
Yes. Participants are welcome to attend the conference even if they are not presenting a contribution.


How will I receive the online access link?
The access link will be sent by email after your registration has been confirmed.
